info.dc:title: Orchestration Defects and Residual-Stream Improvements in the research-development-loop Skill: A First-Iteration Evaluation
info.dc:creator: metavacua (Claude Sonnet 4.6, 2026-06-29)
info.dc:subject: agent orchestration; skill composition; residual stream; scope classification; evaluation methodology; LFI; logic of formal inconsistency; R&D loop; babel-harness
info.dc:description: We evaluate the first iteration of improvements to the research-development-loop orchestrator skill for the babel-harness coding agent. Three changes were applied: a scope-classification guard (Step 0.0) that routes mechanical tasks directly to Phase 3, bypassing Phases 1–2; an Artifact (A) category extending the K/Q/C/E/D residual format for non-propositional verbatim content; and a first-run evaluation suite of three decision-point tests. Empirical results show the scope guard achieves 2.25× speed improvement on mechanical tasks but misclassifies tasks with implicit underspecification. The benchmark registers 83% pass rate for the new skill versus 100% for the baseline — a measurement artifact caused by an assertion design mismatch rather than a true regression. Most critically, the session itself demonstrates the primary defect in the skill: the research-development-loop orchestrator was invoked but its Phase 5 (Scholarly Review) and Phase 6 (Finishing) were never reached because the skill-creator sub-skill captured control without returning it. This paper is Phase 5, executing late after explicit user correction. We report all findings with equal depth for capabilities and defects, per the skill's honesty mandate.
info.bibliomisc: { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "ScholarlyArticle", "name": "Orchestration Defects and Residual-Stream Improvements in the research-development-loop Skill", "datePublished": "2026-06-29", "inLanguage": "en", "license": "https://www.gnu.org/licenses/agpl-3.0.html", "publisher": { "@type": "Organization", "name": "metavacua/babel-harness" }, "keywords": [ "agent orchestration", "skill composition", "residual stream", "scope classification", "logic of formal inconsistency", "R&D loop", "babel-harness", "evaluation methodology" ] }
